title: Wire extra-packages.yaml as Docker build source of truth
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'There is releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml. JEdoc'

Restructure releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml into pip_extras (bare extras: ldap, memcache_pool, oauth1), pip_packages (standalone packages, initially empty), and apt_packages (runtime: libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3, libldap2, libsasl2-2, libxml2) — fixing the incorrect libldap-2.5-0 to libldap2. Add a 'Resolve extra packages' step in .github/workflows/build-images.yaml (build-service-images job, after the existing 'Resolve source ref' step) that uses yq to extract all three fields per service from extra-packages.yaml and writes them to $GITHUB_OUTPUT. Pass these as --build-arg PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, and EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES to docker/build-push-action. In images/keystone/Dockerfile, declare the three ARGs and replace the hardcoded [ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1] with [${PIP_EXTRAS}], add ${PIP_PACKAGES} to uv pip install, and replace the hardcoded apt package list with ${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES}. Update tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh: remove the now-obsolete test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile function and update test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure to validate the new field names (pip_extras, pip_packages, apt_packages), non-empty pip_extras and apt_packages, and valid package name patterns.

**Rationale:** extra-packages.yaml exists but is completely ignored by the build pipeline — packages are independently hardcoded in the Dockerfile, creating silent drift risk and a false sense of configuration. The file also contains libldap-2.5-0 which does not exist as an Ubuntu Noble package (correct name: libldap2). Making the YAML the single source of truth eliminates duplication, prevents drift, and ensures release-specific package declarations actually reach the built images.

**Affected Areas:**
- releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- images/keystone/Dockerfile
- tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh

stories.title: CI pipeline consumes extra-packages.yaml during service image builds
stories.want: the build-images workflow to read pip_extras, pip_packages, and apt_packages from extra-packages.yaml and pass them as Docker build arguments
stories.so_that: extra-packages.yaml is the single source of truth for per-service packages, eliminating silent drift between YAML and Dockerfile
criteria: build-service-images job has a 'Resolve extra packages' step after 'Resolve source ref' that extracts all three fields via yq
Extracted values are written to $GITHUB_OUTPUT as pip-extras, pip-packages, and apt-packages
docker/build-push-action receives --build-arg PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, and EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES
Workflow fails with ::error:: if pip_extras or apt_packages are missing or null for the matrix service

stories.title: Dockerfile accepts package lists via build arguments instead of hardcoding them
stories.want: the keystone Dockerfile to declare ARG PIP_EXTRAS, ARG PIP_PACKAGES, and ARG EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES and use them in the uv pip install and apt-get install commands
stories.so_that: the Dockerfile is release-independent and package lists are injected by the CI pipeline
criteria: Stage 1 (build) declares ARG PIP_EXTRAS and ARG PIP_PACKAGES before the RUN instruction
uv pip install uses /tmp/keystone-build[${PIP_EXTRAS}] instead of hardcoded [ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]
uv pip install includes ${PIP_PACKAGES} as additional install targets (handles empty gracefully)
Stage 2 (runtime) declares ARG EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES before the apt-get RUN instruction
apt-get install uses ${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES} instead of the hardcoded package list
No hardcoded package names remain in the Dockerfile (ldap, memcache_pool, oauth1, libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3, etc.)

stories.title: Release engineer manages per-service packages by editing only extra-packages.yaml
stories.want: extra-packages.yaml to use a clear schema with pip_extras (bare extra names), pip_packages (standalone packages), and apt_packages (runtime system packages)
stories.so_that: I can add, remove, or modify service packages in one place without touching Dockerfiles or workflow files
criteria: pip_extras contains bare extra names (ldap, memcache_pool, oauth1) — not wrapped in keystone[...] format
pip_packages is an empty list (initially) — ready for standalone packages in the future
apt_packages contains valid Ubuntu Noble package names (libldap2 instead of incorrect libldap-2.5-0)
Header comment reflects that this file IS the consumed source of truth (no WARNING about manual sync)

stories.title: CI tests validate the restructured extra-packages.yaml schema
stories.want: verify_release_config.sh to validate the new pip_extras/pip_packages/apt_packages fields and remove the obsolete Dockerfile-matching test
stories.so_that: malformed configurations are caught before builds and the test suite reflects the new architecture
criteria: test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure validates field names pip_extras, pip_packages, apt_packages
Test asserts pip_extras is non-empty and apt_packages is non-empty
Test validates package name patterns (pip_extras: alphanumeric+underscore; apt_packages: valid dpkg names)
test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile function is removed entirely
Test runner no longer calls test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile
Total test count in the script adjusts accordingly

stories.title: Reference documentation reflects the new extra-packages.yaml schema and workflow step
stories.want: docs/reference/container-images.md and docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md to describe the new schema and CI integration
stories.so_that: contributors understand the current format and know that extra-packages.yaml is consumed by the build pipeline
criteria: docs/reference/container-images.md shows the new pip_extras/pip_packages/apt_packages schema with correct examples
docs/reference/container-images.md shows libldap2 (not libldap-2.5-0) in apt_packages
docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md documents the 'Resolve extra packages' step in the build-service-images steps table
docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md documents the build-args passed to docker/build-push-action

requirements.description: releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml SHALL use a restructured schema with pip_extras (bare extra names list), pip_packages (standalone packages list, may be empty), and apt_packages (runtime system packages list) per service
requirements.rationale: The current pip_packages field contains full keystone[extra] entries which conflate service identity with extra names; restructuring into pip_extras (bare names) and pip_packages (standalone) enables clean injection into Dockerfile ARGs

scenarios.name: Schema has correct field names
scenarios.then: the keystone entry has fields pip_extras, pip_packages, and apt_packages
and_then: no pip_packages field exists in the old format (keystone[...] entries)

scenarios.name: pip_extras contains bare extra names
scenarios.then: the list contains bare names: ldap, memcache_pool, oauth1
and_then: no entry contains brackets or the service name prefix

scenarios.name: pip_packages is initially empty
scenarios.then: the list is empty ([])
and_then: the field exists and is a valid YAML sequence

requirements.description: apt_packages in extra-packages.yaml SHALL contain valid Ubuntu Noble package names, correcting libldap-2.5-0 to libldap2
requirements.rationale: libldap-2.5-0 does not exist as an Ubuntu Noble package; the correct runtime package name is libldap2, which is already used in the current Dockerfile

scenarios.name: libldap2 is present
scenarios.then: the list contains libldap2

scenarios.name: libldap-2.5-0 is absent
scenarios.then: the list does NOT contain libldap-2.5-0

scenarios.name: All apt package names match valid dpkg naming pattern
scenarios.when: each entry in keystone.apt_packages is validated
scenarios.then: each name matches the pattern [a-z0-9][a-z0-9.+\-]+ (dpkg package naming convention)

requirements.description: The CI workflow SHALL add a 'Resolve extra packages' step in build-service-images (after 'Resolve source ref') that extracts pip_extras, pip_packages, and apt_packages from extra-packages.yaml via yq and writes them to $GITHUB_OUTPUT
requirements.rationale: Follows the established yq-based resolution pattern from source-ref extraction, ensuring the YAML values reach the Docker build

scenarios.name: yq extracts pip_extras as comma-separated string
scenarios.when: the 'Resolve extra packages' step runs for keystone
scenarios.then: pip-extras output contains 'ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1'

scenarios.name: yq extracts pip_packages as space-separated string
scenarios.when: the 'Resolve extra packages' step runs for keystone
scenarios.then: pip-packages output is empty (no standalone packages yet)

scenarios.name: yq extracts apt_packages as space-separated string
scenarios.when: the 'Resolve extra packages' step runs for keystone
scenarios.then: apt-packages output contains 'libapache2-mod-wsgi-py3 libldap2 libsasl2-2 libxml2'

requirements.description: The CI workflow SHALL fail with a descriptive ::error:: message if pip_extras or apt_packages are missing or null for a matrix service in extra-packages.yaml
requirements.rationale: Silent fallback to empty package lists would produce broken images; fail-fast behavior matches the existing source-ref null-check pattern

scenarios.name: Missing pip_extras fails the step
scenarios.when: extra-packages.yaml has no pip_extras field for the matrix service
scenarios.then: the step exits with code 1 and emits ::error:: with the service name and file path

scenarios.name: Null pip_extras fails the step
scenarios.when: extra-packages.yaml has pip_extras: null for the matrix service
scenarios.then: the step exits with code 1 and emits ::error:: with the service name and file path

scenarios.name: Missing apt_packages fails the step
scenarios.when: extra-packages.yaml has no apt_packages field for the matrix service
scenarios.then: the step exits with code 1 and emits ::error:: with the service name and file path

requirements.description: The CI workflow SHALL pass PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, and EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES as --build-arg values to docker/build-push-action for service image builds
requirements.rationale: Docker build arguments are the mechanism to inject CI-resolved values into the Dockerfile without hardcoding

scenarios.name: build-args are present in docker/build-push-action
scenarios.when: the 'Build service image' step is configured
scenarios.then: it includes build-args with PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, and EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES referencing the extra-packages step outputs

scenarios.name: Empty PIP_PACKAGES is handled gracefully
scenarios.then: PIP_PACKAGES build-arg is passed as an empty string
and_then: the Dockerfile uv pip install command handles the empty value without error

scenarios.name: build-args reference correct step ID
scenarios.then: they use steps.extra-packages.outputs.pip-extras, steps.extra-packages.outputs.pip-packages, steps.extra-packages.outputs.apt-packages

requirements.description: The keystone Dockerfile SHALL declare ARG PIP_EXTRAS, ARG PIP_PACKAGES, ARG EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES and use them in place of all hardcoded package lists
requirements.rationale: Replacing hardcoded values with ARGs makes the Dockerfile fully parameterized by CI, eliminating drift between YAML and build

scenarios.name: Build stage uses PIP_EXTRAS for extras
scenarios.when: the Dockerfile build stage runs uv pip install
scenarios.then: it uses /tmp/keystone-build[${PIP_EXTRAS}] instead of hardcoded [ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]

scenarios.name: Build stage includes PIP_PACKAGES as additional targets
scenarios.when: the Dockerfile build stage runs uv pip install
scenarios.then: it includes ${PIP_PACKAGES} as additional install targets after the service[extras] argument
and_then: empty PIP_PACKAGES results in no additional packages being installed

scenarios.name: Runtime stage uses EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES
scenarios.when: the Dockerfile runtime stage runs apt-get install
scenarios.then: it uses ${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES} instead of the hardcoded package list
and_then: no hardcoded apt package names remain in the Dockerfile

requirements.description: test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure in verify_release_config.sh SHALL validate the new field names (pip_extras, pip_packages, apt_packages), non-empty pip_extras and apt_packages, and valid package name patterns
requirements.rationale: Tests must validate the new schema to prevent regressions; the old field-name-based assertions must be replaced

scenarios.name: Test validates pip_extras field exists and is non-empty
scenarios.then: it asserts keystone.pip_extras has at least 1 item

scenarios.name: Test validates apt_packages field exists and is non-empty
scenarios.then: it asserts keystone.apt_packages has at least 1 item

scenarios.name: Test validates pip_extras entries are bare names
scenarios.then: it asserts each pip_extras entry matches ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ (no brackets or service prefix)

scenarios.name: Test validates apt_packages entries match dpkg naming
scenarios.then: it asserts each apt_packages entry matches ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.+\-]+$ (valid dpkg package name)

requirements.description: The obsolete test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile function SHALL be removed from verify_release_config.sh
requirements.rationale: Since the Dockerfile no longer hardcodes packages, comparing YAML entries to Dockerfile content is meaningless and would fail

scenarios.name: Function is removed
scenarios.then: test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile function does not exist

scenarios.name: Function is not called
scenarios.when: the test runner section of verify_release_config.sh executes
scenarios.then: test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile is not invoked

scenarios.name: Test script still passes
scenarios.then: exit code is 0 with updated pass count (no reference to missing function)

requirements.description: Reference documentation SHALL be updated to describe the new extra-packages.yaml schema and the 'Resolve extra packages' CI workflow step
requirements.rationale: Outdated documentation describing the old pip_packages format and missing the CI integration step would mislead contributors

scenarios.name: container-images.md shows new schema
scenarios.then: the extra-packages.yaml section shows pip_extras, pip_packages, and apt_packages fields
and_then: libldap2 is shown instead of libldap-2.5-0
pip_extras shows bare names, not keystone[extra] format

scenarios.name: build-images-workflow.md documents new step
scenarios.then: the build-service-images steps table includes 'Resolve extra packages' after 'Resolve source ref'
and_then: build-args section documents PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES

scenarios.name: container-images.md Dockerfile example updated
scenarios.when: docs/reference/container-images.md keystone Dockerfile section is read
scenarios.then: the uv pip install line shows ARG-based extras, not hardcoded [ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1]
and_then: the apt-get install line shows ARG-based packages, not hardcoded list

Update images/keystone/Dockerfile: declare ARG PIP_EXTRAS and ARG PIP_PACKAGES in build stage, ARG EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES in runtime stage; replace hardcoded [ldap,memcache_pool,oauth1] with [${PIP_EXTRAS}], add ${PIP_PACKAGES} to uv pip install, replace hardcoded apt package list with ${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES} (REQ-006)

Add 'Resolve extra packages' step in .github/workflows/build-images.yaml build-service-images job after 'Resolve source ref': use yq to extract pip_extras (join with comma), pip_packages (join with space), apt_packages (join with space) from extra-packages.yaml, write to $GITHUB_OUTPUT with null/empty checks and ::error:: messages (REQ-003, REQ-004)
Add build-args (PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES) to the docker/build-push-action step in build-service-images job, referencing steps.extra-packages.outputs (REQ-005)

Update test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure in tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh: validate new field names (pip_extras, pip_packages, apt_packages), assert pip_extras count >= 1 and apt_packages count >= 1, add pattern validation for pip_extras entries (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$) and apt_packages entries (^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.+-]+$) (REQ-007)
Remove test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile function and its invocation from tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh (REQ-008)

Update docs/reference/container-images.md: replace extra-packages.yaml section with new pip_extras/pip_packages/apt_packages schema, update example YAML, fix libldap-2.5-0 to libldap2, update keystone Dockerfile example to show ARG-based usage, update field description table (REQ-009)

Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md: add 'Resolve extra packages' step to build-service-images steps table (step 3, after 'Resolve source ref'), document build-args (PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES) in the Build Contexts or a new Build Arguments section, update step numbering (REQ-009)

review_criteria: extra-packages.yaml uses pip_extras (bare names), pip_packages (empty list), apt_packages — old pip_packages format with keystone[...] entries is gone
apt_packages contains libldap2, not libldap-2.5-0; all package names are valid dpkg names installable on Ubuntu Noble
CI workflow 'Resolve extra packages' step follows the same yq + null-check + $GITHUB_OUTPUT pattern as 'Resolve source ref'
CI workflow passes all three build-args (PIP_EXTRAS, PIP_PACKAGES, EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES) to docker/build-push-action
Dockerfile contains no hardcoded package names — all package lists come from ARG variables; empty PIP_PACKAGES does not break uv pip install
test_extra_packages_match_dockerfile is completely removed (function definition and invocation); test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure validates new field names and patterns
verify_release_config.sh passes (exit 0) with all assertions succeeding for the restructured YAML
docs/reference/container-images.md and docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md reflect the new schema and CI step without referencing the old format
implementation_notes:
Architectural decisions and patterns:

1. **yq extraction pattern**: Follow the established `source-ref` resolution pattern in build-images.yaml (env vars for matrix values, yq query, null check, $GITHUB_OUTPUT). The new step should use the same structure including the descriptive echo at the end.

2. **Join strategy for yq output**: pip_extras use comma-join (`.pip_extras | join(",")`) because they become a single [extra1,extra2] bracket expression. pip_packages and apt_packages use space-join (`.pip_packages | join(" ")` / `.apt_packages | join(" ")`) because they are separate shell arguments.

3. **Dockerfile ARG placement**: PIP_EXTRAS and PIP_PACKAGES must be declared in Stage 1 (build) before the RUN instruction that uses them. EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES must be declared in Stage 2 (runtime) before the apt-get RUN instruction. ARGs are stage-scoped in multi-stage builds.

4. **Empty PIP_PACKAGES handling**: When PIP_PACKAGES is empty, the uv pip install command becomes `uv pip install ... "/tmp/keystone-build[${PIP_EXTRAS}]" ` — the trailing empty string is harmless because shell word splitting on an unquoted empty variable produces no additional arguments.

5. **EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES must be unquoted**: The apt-get install command uses `${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES}` (unquoted) to allow shell word splitting into separate package arguments. This is intentional — quoting would pass the entire space-separated string as a single argument to apt.

6. **Header comment update**: Replace the WARNING block in extra-packages.yaml with a comment stating this file is the consumed source of truth for per-service packages in the Docker build.

7. **Architecture doc divergence**: architecture/docs/08-container-images/01-build-pipeline.md still references libldap-2.5-0 in its example — this is read-only reference material and is NOT modified by this feature.

Key files:
- releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml (restructure schema)
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml (add step + build-args, lines ~96-189)
- images/keystone/Dockerfile (ARGs + variable substitution)
- tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh (update + remove tests)
- tests/lib/assertions.sh (existing assertion helpers — use assert_eq)
- docs/reference/container-images.md (update schema docs)
- docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md (add step docs)

Risks:
- If yq is not available in the CI runner, the 'Resolve extra packages' step will fail. Mitigation: yq is already used by the existing 'Resolve source ref' step, so it is guaranteed to be available.
- If PIP_EXTRAS is empty and no null check fires, the Dockerfile would install keystone[] which may cause uv to error. Mitigation: REQ-004 requires fail-fast on empty/null pip_extras.
- The unquoted ${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES} in apt-get install relies on shell word splitting. If a package name contained spaces (invalid for dpkg), it would break. Mitigation: REQ-007 validates package names match dpkg naming conventions (no spaces).
